Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Switch Daytona to pre-warmed sandbox pool for faster boot and better security

Use CreateSandboxBaseParams(language='python') instead of
CreateSandboxFromImageParams with custom images. This gives 0.17s stock
boot (was ~1.3s), non-root execution (uid=1001), zero capabilities, and
filtered egress (security 7/8, was 6/8). Updated all 13 files with
Daytona path changes (/root/ → /home/daytona/) to match pre-warmed pool
user. Updated FULL_BENCHMARK_REPORT.md with all latest benchmark results.

commit.message:
Enhancement: Deduplicate seeded RNG — replace private LCG with shared createSeededRng() (#1722) (#1729)

* Enhancement: Deduplicate seeded RNG — replace private LCG with shared createSeededRng()

Remove duplicate private createSeededRandom() LCG implementations from
BrittlenessScorer.ts and HoldoutValidator.ts, replacing them with the
shared xoshiro256** PRNG via createSeededRng() from RandomNumberGenerator.ts.

Closes #1722

Fix: recalibrar sceneEV para que coincidan con la fórmula log2(N²/t)+log2(ISO/100)

Los sceneEV anteriores (5-9) no tenían en cuenta que la fórmula suma log2(ISO/100)
al cameraEV. Con ISO 1600 eso es +4 EV, causando que todos los presets mostraran
advertencia de sobreexposición al aplicarlos. Valores corregidos: 12-14.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(agents): add claude:auto_advance label for pipeline gating (#92)

## Summary

- Add `claude:auto_advance` label that controls whether the
design→review→implement pipeline runs autonomously or pauses for human
approval
- **With label**: Pipeline auto-advances (current behavior) — used by
engineer managers
- **Without label**: Pipeline pauses after design and after review,
waiting for human to apply the next label

## Changes

- **`agentic-designer.md`**: Check for `claude:auto_advance` before
applying `claude:review`. Without it, pause and ask human to review.
- **`architect.md`**: Check for `claude:auto_advance` before applying
`claude:implement`. Without it, pause and inform human design is
approved.
- **`00-engineer-base.md`**: Engineer managers apply
`claude:auto_advance` alongside the delegation label when creating
issues. Also ensure the label is created during dashboard setup.

## Motivation

Issue #89 demonstrated the problem: a human-created issue went through
the entire design→review→implement pipeline in 5 minutes with zero human
input. The designer auto-triggered the architect, which auto-triggered
the developer, which opened a PR — all before the human could review the
design.

## Test plan

- [ ] Create an issue manually, comment `@claude` — verify pipeline
pauses after design
- [ ] Apply `claude:review` manually — verify architect pauses after
review
- [ ] Apply `claude:implement` manually — verify developer runs
- [ ] Create an issue with `claude:auto_advance` label — verify full
auto pipeline
- [ ] Verify engineer managers still apply `claude:auto_advance` when
delegating

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix COOP-blocked OAuth and empty backup destination dropdown

OAuth fix (COOP issue):
- Callback pages now ALWAYS write result to localStorage first, then
  try window.opener.postMessage as fallback
- Parent windows (index.html, settings.html) now listen for BOTH
  window.message AND localStorage storage events to handle result
- This fixes 'callback_processing_failed' caused by Google COOP headers
  nullifying window.opener in the popup

Backup destination fix:
- Remove aggressive auto-reset in handleDestinationChange that was
  resetting select to 'local' when BackupMonitor.googleDriveConnected
  was false (conflicted with settings.html's own connected state)
- Now only shows a warning, lets user keep their selection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 500 on approve-final: transaction rollback from usage tracking

Root cause: when claim.clientId is a FamilyMember ID (not a Client),
ClientUsageService.incrementUsage threw NotFoundException inside a
@Transactional method. Spring marked the outer transaction as
rollback-only. Even though approveAdmin caught the exception, the
subsequent claimRepo.save() failed with UnexpectedRollbackException.

Fix:
- Add resolveClientId() to map FamilyMember IDs to parent Client IDs
- Use Propagation.REQUIRES_NEW on incrementUsage/decrementUsage so
  failures don't poison the caller's transaction
- Gracefully return (no-op) if client ID can't be resolved

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix bucket value overwrite by global position value

the global deduped total. Swap spread order so h (bucket value) wins.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(lmx): resolve venv symlink and buffer limit issues in CoreML backend

Two bugs found during Mono512 integration testing:

1. _find_coreml_python() used Path.resolve() which follows symlinks,
   destroying venv context (venv python3 → bare system Python that
   lacks coremltools/numpy). Fix: use expanduser() only.

2. asyncio default 64KB stream buffer too small for batch embedding
   responses (10 vectors × 384 floats ≈ 40KB+ JSON). Fix: set
   limit=1MB on subprocess stream.

Verified: all 4 integration tests pass on Mono512 (750 emb/sec batch).

commit.message:
perf: Phase B prefill alpha — HiPerf GEMM, merged projections, ExecGraph (#58)

* perf: Phase B prefill alpha — HiPerf GEMM, merged projections, ExecGraph prefill

GEMM kernel restructure:
- New gemm_hiperf_f16: 128 threads (4 simdgroups), BM=64 BN=64 BK=16
- Each SG computes 32x32 output (16 MMA accumulators vs 4 previously)
- 4x deeper MMA pipeline utilization per simdgroup
- Single-buffered 4KB shmem (vs 16KB double-buffered)

Merged projections:
- batched_qkv_proj_into_cb: Q/K/V GEMMs in single encoder flow
- batched_gate_up_into_cb: gate+up GEMMs in single encoder flow
- Reduces compute encoder creation overhead

ExecGraph prefill:
- TransformerBlock::forward_prefill_graph: 1 CB/layer with GPU-side events
- TransformerModel::forward_prefill_graph: full model with single CPU sync
- Benchmark updated with 3-way comparison (forward vs single_cb vs graph)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update documentation for Phase B prefill alpha optimizations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: revert HiPerf GEMM dispatch (regression) + fix ExecGraph bench event bug

- GEMM: revert Full+f16 dispatch to gemm_tiled_f16 (HiPerf regressed 10T vs 13.4T)
- Bench: create new GpuEvent per iteration to prevent stale event value

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: GEMM vectorized loads (half4) + HiPerf v2 double-buffered kernel

- gemm_tiled_f16: vectorized half4 loads for A/B tile prefetch (4x memory throughput)
- gemm_tiled_f16: fast-path simdgroup_store when 8x8 output block fully in-bounds
- gemm_hiperf_f16: double-buffered shared memory (8KB) for memory latency hiding
- Dispatch Full+f16 to gemm_hiperf_f16 (128 threads, 4 SG, 16 MMA accumulators)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: revert dispatch to gemm_tiled_f16 (HiPerf v2 regressed to ~11T)

4 SG / 128 threads insufficient occupancy on Apple Silicon.
Keep vectorized half4 loads in gemm_tiled_f16 (8 SG / 256 threads).
HiPerf v2 kernel source retained for future experimentation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: GEMM tile coverage bug — SG grid was 2x4 (32x64) instead of 4x2 (64x64)

Critical correctness fix: with SG_ROWS=2, SG_COLS=4, each SG computing
16x16 output, only rows 0-31 were computed for a BM=64 tile. Rows 32-63
were never written, producing incorrect results for M>32 Full tile path.

Fix: SG_ROWS=4, SG_COLS=2, acc[2][4] — each SG now computes 16x32 output.
Total coverage: 4×16=64 rows, 2×32=64 cols = full 64×64 tile.
Applied to f32, f16, and bf16 kernels.

Also adds large-matrix correctness tests (test_matmul_large_f32/f16).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: f32 GEMM threadgroup memory overflow — reuse As buffer for result_buf

f32 kernel used 34KB threadgroup memory (As 16KB + Bs 16KB + result_buf 2KB)
exceeding Metal's 32KB limit. Reuse As[0] as scratch during store phase
since shared memory is no longer needed after compute loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: fix rustfmt + clippy warnings for Phase B CI

- cargo fmt --all (fused.rs, ops_tests.rs, prefill_bench.rs, transformer.rs)
- clippy: &mut Vec<LayerKvCache> → &mut [LayerKvCache]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

fix: address security audit findings (#197)

* fix: address security audit findings across auth, logging, analytics, and privacy

- Replace fatalError() with thrown AuthError in Apple Sign-In nonce generation
- Clean up nonce and continuation after auth completes to prevent reuse/leaks
- Log warning for unknown auth providers instead of silent default
- Remove environment variable key dump from API key error logging
- Redact search queries from debug logs (log count + length only)
- Set isAuthenticating flag before async Task to close race window
- Sanitize error context (emails, URL params) before sending to Crashlytics
- Update privacy manifest with crash data, user ID, search/reading history
- Add Content-Security-Policy meta tag to WKWebView YouTube embed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review comments

- Add google.com to CSP script-src for YouTube IFrame API compatibility
- Use defer for cleanupAppleSignInState inside Task for robustness
- Use BrowsingHistory instead of OtherUsageData for reading history in privacy manifest

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use coordinate-based tap for Media tab in UI tests

iOS 26 Liquid Glass tab bar causes XCTest's tap() to time out
when evaluating the UI query for hittability. Using coordinate-based
tapping bypasses this evaluation and resolves the flaky
testMediaAccessibilityAudit failure.
